content_preview.title: The World's Highest Submillimeter Telescope Opens Its Eyes on the Atacama After Three Decades in the Making
content_preview.summary: The Fred Young Submillimeter Telescope, perched at 5,600 meters in Chile, inaugurated on April 9 with over 100,000 superconducting detectors and a mapping speed ten times faster than any predecessor.
content_preview.body_excerpt:
## Overview

On April 9, more than a hundred scientists, engineers, and dignitaries gathered on the 5,600-meter summit of Cerro Chajnantor in Chile's Atacama Desert to inaugurate the Fred Young Submillimeter Telescope (FYST), according to [Universe Today](https://www.universetoday.com/articles/a-new-eye-opens-at-the-top-of-the-world). The six-meter instrument, roughly three decades in the making, is now the highest permanent astronomical observatory on Earth and the most powerful submillimeter s...
